info.description: This problem set focuses on usage and mechanisms of antibacterial agents. The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: Which antibacterial agent exerts its effect by binding to the D-Ala-D-Ala terminus of nascent peptidoglycan pentapeptides, thereby sterically hindering peptidoglycan cross-linking and polymerization?

q2.prompt: A 24-year-old woman presents with a thin, grayish-white vaginal discharge and a "fishy" odor that intensifies after adding KOH to the sample. Which of the following antibacterial is used as the drug of choice for this condition and act by mechanisms involving DNAs?

q3.prompt: A 23-week pregnant patient requires treatment for an acute uncomplicated cystitis. Which of the following drugs is appropriate for this patient?

q4.prompt: A member of which class of antibacterial agents inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis by interfering with the movement of peptidoglycan precursors across the cytoplasmic membrane?

q5.prompt: A 7-year-old child presents with a localized skin infection caused by Methicillin-Susceptible Staphylococcus aureus (MSSA). Which of the following is a first-line oral antibiotic for this condition?

q6.prompt: A patient undergoing treatment for *Mycobacterium tuberculosis* complains that their tears and urine have turned an orange-red colour. Which bacterial enzyme is specifically inhibited by the antibiotic responsible for this side effect?

q7.prompt: A 12-year-old boy requires treatment for a suspected Gram-negative infection. Which antibiotic class should be avoided in this patient due to its potential to cause damage to developing cartilage and tendons?

q8.prompt: Which of the following β-lactam antibiotics is unique among cephalosporins for its clinical activity against Methicillin-Resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA)?

q9.prompt: Which antibiotic prevents the formation of the bacterial initiation complex by specifically binding to the 50S ribosomal subunit?

q10.prompt: Azithromycin is frequently used to treat atypical pneumonia caused by *Mycoplasma pneumoniae*. Which of the following best describes its primary mechanism of action?

options.label: Prevention of polypeptide elongation at the 50S ribosome
Inhibition of DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV
Binding to the 30S ribosomal subunit to block mRNA translation
Inhibition of cell wall peptidoglycan synthesis
Disruption of the bacterial cell membrane integrity
